findings.message: httpx is a reputable, actively maintained HTTP client (encode/httpx). The upper bound <1.0 is good practice to guard against breaking changes at the v1 boundary. No supply-chain concerns.

Version floor mismatch: runtime dep allows httpx>=0.27 but the dev dependency-group pins httpx>=0.28. Tests always run against 0.28+, so a production install of 0.27 could expose untested behaviour differences between those minor releases.
findings.suggestion: Align the runtime lower bound with the dev lower bound: "httpx>=0.28,<1.0" so that the tested version range matches what ships to production.
